nodes.label: Stable Shell / Volatile Engines
nodes.rationale: Infrastructure is boring and should stay that way. The experiments are meant to break, evolve, and get rewritten.

nodes.label: Stateless First
nodes.rationale: Avoid state until you actually need it. Pure functions > side effects.

nodes.label: Composable UI
nodes.rationale: Small, focused components that do one thing well. Mix and match.

nodes.label: Monorepo Architecture
nodes.rationale: Clear separation between the shell and the engines. The shell provides layout and navigation. Engines own their logic. UI components bridge them.

nodes.label: Sequence Renderer Engine
nodes.rationale: Rules generate data, visualizations render it. Rules define what numbers to generate. Visualizations define how to draw them. They are independent — mix any rule with any visualization.

nodes.label: Pluggable Architecture
nodes.rationale: Add new rules or visualizations by: defining the data structure, adding to the registry array, and the UI updates automatically. This keeps the engine core stable while experiments evolve freely.

nodes.label: Explicit Named Exports
nodes.rationale: Prevent accidental API growth, reduce export name collisions, make reviews and refactors safer, keep package contracts readable.

nodes.label: Standard Component Variants
nodes.rationale: Components follow a shared variant system: primary, secondary, accent, destructive, outline, ghost — providing visual consistency across the UI library.
